title: Warm Hakka Beetroot Sweet And Sour
description: A floral and chewy Hakka dish featuring cooked beetroot in a sweet and sour sauce, cooked in a cataplana vessel and wrapped in papillote, making it ayurvedic-rajasic-friendly.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together rice vinegar, sugar, water, and cornstarch.
In a cataplana vessel, heat the vegetable oil over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger to the vessel and cook for 1 minute.
Add the diced beetroot to the vessel and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Pour the vinegar mixture into the vessel and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in the sesame oil and salt.
Wrap the vessel in papillote and bake for 15 minutes.
Remove the vessel from the oven and let it cool for 5 minutes.
Serve the Warm Hakka Beetroot Sweet And Sour hot, garnished with chopped scallions if desired.
